title: syngramma quinata
intro: Syngramma quinata, also known as the Five-angled Fern or Quinata Fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its distinctive fronds with five to seven leaflets. The medicinal properties of Syngramma quinata lie in its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive problems and skin infections. In herbal preparations, Syngramma quinata is often dried and used as a tea, or made into a decoction by infusing the leaves in hot water.
